steps.title: Pin down the claim
steps.content: The claim: Newland compares his intellect favorably to that of other men in the audience. The supporting quote needs to show Newland viewing his own mind as superior to theirs.

steps.title: Find the comparative quote
steps.content:
A is about opera-translation conventions. B is about masculine solidarity. D is about the audience's brilliance generally — none compares Newland's intellect to others'.

C says Newland felt distinctly the superior of these men, having read more, thought more — a direct favorable intellectual comparison. C is correct.
